description: R (Classify Send Attempt, workflow 03) treats an HTTP 2xx response from Q (POST /api/v2/emails/reply) as terminal SENT only when the response body is a valid Email object: non-empty string id, message_id, and thread_id. Any 2xx response missing one of these fields is SEND_UNCERTAIN (ambiguous, never retried).

V (Reconciliation Poll, workflow 03) issues a GET to https://api.instantly.ai/api/v2/emails with search/eaccount/lead/email_type=sent/min_timestamp_created/max_timestamp_created/preview_only=false/limit query params. W (Process Reconciliation Poll) locally filters the returned items and reconciles to SENT_RECONCILED only after two consecutive single-match polls of the same id; zero matches or multiple matches route to HUMAN_REVIEW_*.

F (Safety Action Plan, workflow 02) emits a schema_version 1.1 plan with exactly 4 actions (SOURCE_CAMPAIGN_STOP, UPDATE_INTEREST_STATUS, SUBSEQUENCE_REMOVAL, EXACT_EMAIL_BLOCKLIST), each with its own request_contract {method,url,body}. The G0..G6 chain gates execution of each action individually by suppression_action_enablement.*; with the shipped config (all disabled), every action remains enabled=false / executed=false / verification_status=SUPPRESSION_ACTION_DISABLED_BY_CONFIG and G6 reports verification_status=EXECUTION_AND_VERIFICATION_GATED_OFFLINE.

apply-business-ready.ps1 refuses to run unless config.owner_inputs_status === 'COMPLETE' and no <REQUIRED_*> placeholders remain (Assert-OwnerInputsComplete), then re-embeds config.launch_profile / config.allowlists / config.reviewer_allowlist / config.suppression_action_enablement verbatim into the HMZ_INJECT-marked LAUNCH_PROFILE (wf03 O), ALLOWLISTS (wf01 F1), REVIEWER_ALLOWLIST (wf07 M1), and SUPPRESSION_ACTION_ENABLEMENT (wf02 F) constants, re-verifying the remote copies afterwards (Assert-InjectedConstantsMatchConfig). Test-AllowedHttpUrl additionally allows the per-action suppression executors (G1h-G4h) whose URL is an expression derived from safety_action_plan.actions[].request_contract.url.

expected.Assert-OwnerInputsComplete: throws when owner_inputs_status !== 'COMPLETE' or a <REQUIRED_*> placeholder remains
expected.Assert-WorkflowBodySafe(workflow_02): does not throw (G1h-G4h request_contract.url expressions are recognised as gated Instantly adapters)

description: run-controlled-live-acceptance.ps1 -AllowOneControlledReply -ConfirmationPhrase 'RUN-ONE-CONTROLLED-REPLY' performs the single controlled live reply by POSTing a synthetic NES payload to the Intake workflow's dev webhook (hmz-validation-reply-intake-dev), temporarily activating the Intake workflow only, then polling n8n's executions API for the Sender workflow's run and reading terminal.send_state / terminal.email_id / terminal.message_id / terminal.thread_id. It never calls POST /api/v2/emails/reply directly, and restores the Intake workflow's prior active state plus dry_run=true / live_campaigns=[] in a finally block regardless of outcome.

F1 (Production Security & Allowlist Gate, workflow 01) is a passthrough for non-PRODUCTION_WEBHOOK entries, but for PRODUCTION_WEBHOOK entries it fails closed against the embedded ALLOWLISTS constant: campaign and sender-eaccount allowlisting are conditional on workspace_id first being allowlisted, so a missing/non-allowlisted workspace_id alone is sufficient to fail CAMPAIGN_NOT_ALLOWLISTED_OR_WORKSPACE_NOT_ALLOWLISTED and SENDER_EACCOUNT_NOT_ALLOWLISTED_OR_WORKSPACE_NOT_ALLOWLISTED as well as WORKSPACE_NOT_ALLOWLISTED. F2 routes passed=false to F3, which builds terminal.result=REJECTED, reason=PRODUCTION_SECURITY_GATE_FAILED.

The production review-form/review-submit webhooks (workflow 07) require Basic Auth via credentialPlaceholder hmzReviewBasicAuth (dev webhooks remain unauthenticated). M1 (Reviewer Identity Allowlist Check) is fail-closed: with the shipped empty REVIEWER_ALLOWLIST, configured=false and allowlisted=false (never null). M2 (Reviewer Allowlist Router) routes allowlisted=true to N (Process Reviewer Decision) and allowlisted=false to M2b (Mark Reviewer Not Allowlisted) -> N2 (Render Submit Token Error), with token_invalid_reason=REVIEWER_NOT_ALLOWLISTED.

run-local-runtime-acceptance.ps1 temporarily activates the Reply Sender, Error Handler, Human Approval, and SLA Watchdog workflows for an isolated window (main n8n stopped), runs the Full Test Harness, then additionally runs standalone `n8n execute` for the Error Handler's real Error Trigger entry node and the SLA Watchdog's real Schedule Trigger entry node, checking persisted_error.errorId and watchdog_result.schema_version/phase4b_result_id respectively. The finally block restarts n8n and deactivates every temporarily-activated workflow, verifying allWorkflowsInactiveAfter=true.
